title: carmichaelia glabrescens
intro: Carmichaelia glabrescens, also known as the "broom flower" or "white broom," is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing pain. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, for treating various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
